title: Slow-cooker BBQ chicken
subtitle: Chicken thighs in a smoky barbecue sauce
excerpt: Boneless chicken thighs slow-cooked in a smoky tomato sauce with smoked paprika, cider vinegar, and Worcestershire sauce for six hours. Pull the meat and return it to the sauce. Serve in buns with coleslaw or over rice.
sourceNotes: Barbecue-style chicken became a British weeknight standard via American food culture in the 2000s. The slow cooker version builds the barbecue sauce from scratch rather than using a bottled version, producing a result that is less sweet and more balanced. The pulled texture from slow-cooked thighs is particularly suited to this preparation.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes extremely well. Pull the chicken, mix with the sauce, and freeze in portions.
recipe.batchNotes: Double the recipe and freeze in portions for quick weeknight meals.

content.text: Pull the chicken after cooking rather than serving the thighs whole. The shredded texture absorbs the sauce more thoroughly and is better suited to buns. Use two forks to pull the meat apart directly in the slow cooker, then stir everything together before serving.

Add the {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{tomato-puree}}, {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{light-brown-sugar}}, {{worcestershire-sauce}}, {{cider-vinegar}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} to the slow cooker and stir to combine. Add the {{chicken-thighs-boneless}} and push them into the sauce. Cook on low for 6 hours, until the chicken is very tender. Using two forks, pull the chicken apart directly in the pot and stir through the sauce. Taste and adjust seasoning — add more cider vinegar for sharpness or more sugar for sweetness.

items.cause: Brown sugar too high, or reduced significantly
items.fix: Add more cider vinegar a teaspoon at a time until balanced. BBQ sauce should have a clear sweet-sour balance

items.cause: Chicken released a lot of liquid
items.fix: Remove the lid and cook on high for 20 minutes to reduce. Alternatively, lift the pulled chicken out and reduce the sauce separately in a pan
Add another teaspoon of smoked paprika and stir. Taste before adding more

content.text: BBQ pulled chicken is a weeknight crowd-pleaser. Serve in toasted brioche buns with coleslaw and pickles, or over rice with corn. The pulled chicken keeps in the fridge for three days and reheats well with a splash of water in a covered pan.
